{"id":"hypercerts-org-v3-6dy.6","title":"Build Partner Logos marquee component","description":"## Files
- components/PartnerLogos.tsx (create — client component with "use client")
- app/page.tsx (modify — add PartnerLogos after HeroSection)

## What to do
Create an auto-scrolling horizontal marquee of partner logos, matching the current site behavior.

### Partner logos data (in order):
```ts
const partners = [
  { href: "https://www.protocol.ai", img: "/img/protocollabs_logo.png", alt: "Protocol Labs" },
  { href: "https://www.optimism.io/", img: "/img/optimism_logo.png", alt: "Optimism" },
  { href: "https://octant.build/", img: "/img/octant_logo.png", alt: "Octant" },
  { href: "https://www.gitcoin.co/", img: "/img/gitcoin_logo.png", alt: "Gitcoin" },
  { href: "https://fundingthecommons.io/", img: "/img/fundingthecommons_logo.png", alt: "Funding the Commons" },
  { href: "https://www.opensource.observer/", img: "/img/opensourceobserver_logo.png", alt: "Open Source Observer" },
  { href: "https://www.raidguild.org/", img: "/img/raidguild_logo.webp", alt: "RaidGuild" },
  { href: "https://www.zuzalu.city/", img: "/img/zuzalu_logo.png", alt: "Zuzalu" },
  { href: "https://www.edgecity.live/", img: "/img/edgecity_logo.png", alt: "EdgeCity" },
  { href: "https://fabdaotw.github.io/", img: "/img/greensofa_logo.png", alt: "GreenSofa" },
];
```

### Behavior:
- Container: full width, 50px height, white bg, overflow-hidden
- Inner row: absolute positioned, flex row, auto-scrolling left at 0.5px per frame using requestAnimationFrame
- Triple the logos array ([...logos, ...logos, ...logos]) for seamless loop
- When scroll reaches 1/3 of total width, reset to 0
- Each logo: 150px wide, 50px tall container, flex centered, white bg, rounded-lg, 1px black border, grayscale filter on image
- Hover: scale(1.1) with transition
- Links open in new tab (target="_blank", rel="noopener noreferrer")
- Clean up animation on unmount (cancelAnimationFrame)

### This is a client component — must have "use client" directive at top.

## Do not
- Use any animation library (CSS or JS only)
- Use CSS keyframe animations (use requestAnimationFrame like the old site)

{"id":"hypercerts-org-v3-yvk.1","title":"Create SVG guilloche pattern component","description":"## Files
- components/Guilloche.tsx (create — client component)

## What to do
Create a reusable SVG guilloche/spirograph pattern component that generates decorative certificate-like line art programmatically. This is the "visual heart" of the Hypercerts brand.

### Component API:
```tsx
interface GuillocheProps {
  width?: number;       // SVG width, default 600
  height?: number;      // SVG height, default 600
  color?: string;       // stroke color, default "#33B899" (brand teal)
  opacity?: number;     // stroke opacity, default 0.3
  className?: string;   // additional CSS classes
  variant?: "radial" | "wave";  // pattern type
}
```

### Variant "radial" (default):
Generate a spirograph-like pattern using parametric equations. Draw multiple overlapping elliptical curves rotated at different angles to create a guilloche rosette. Use SVG `<path>` elements with thin strokes (0.5-1px), no fill.

Algorithm: For i in 0..numPetals (e.g. 12):
- Rotate by (i * 360/numPetals) degrees
- Draw an elliptical arc or bezier curve
- The result should look like the concentric petal patterns in the Figma brand guidelines

### Variant "wave":
Generate horizontal wavy lines that undulate across the width. Use sine-wave based paths with varying amplitudes. Draw 15-20 parallel wavy lines with slight phase offsets. This creates the wave-like guilloche pattern seen in the moodboard.

### Styling:
- SVG should be absolutely positioned (the parent controls placement)
- pointer-events: none (decorative only)
- Use the color prop for stroke, no fill
- Lines should be thin and elegant (strokeWidth 0.5-1)

### Implementation notes:
- Generate paths mathematically in the component (NOT hardcoded SVG strings)
- Use "use client" since we compute paths
- The component should be lightweight — no animation, just static SVG
- Export as default

## Do not
- Use any SVG library (d3, etc.) — pure math + SVG elements
- Add animation or interactivity
- Make it responsive (parent handles sizing via className)","acceptance_criteria":"1. components/Guilloche.tsx exists with "use client" directive

- app/layout.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the `<html>` and `<body>` tags in the root layout. Browser extensions (like LanguageTool) inject attributes into these elements on the client side, causing hydration mismatches. This is the standard Next.js fix.

Change line 44 from:
```tsx
<html lang="en">
```
to:
```tsx
<html lang="en" suppressHydrationWarning>
```

And change line 45 from:
```tsx
<body className="font-body">
```
to:
```tsx
<body className="font-body" suppressHydrationWarning>
```

{"id":"hypercerts-org-v3-do9.8","title":"Fix hamburger menu X animation alignment","description":"## Files
- components/Header.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The hamburger icon has 3 bars with gap-1.5 (6px gap). When open, the top bar uses translate-y-2 (8px) and bottom bar uses -translate-y-2 (8px), but the actual distance between bars is: bar-height(2px) + gap(6px) = 8px total between centers of adjacent bars, so center-to-center from top to bottom is 16px.

The correct translation to form an X: each outer bar needs to move to the center bars position. With gap-1.5 and h-0.5 bars:
- Top bar center to middle bar center = 0.5*2px + 6px + 0.5*2px = 8px = translate-y-[8px]
- Bottom bar center to middle bar center = same = -translate-y-[8px]

Current translate-y-2 = 8px which is correct. The issue is actually that the bars are using h-0.5 (2px height) and the gap is gap-1.5 (6px). So the total offset from first bar to second bar center is 2px + 6px = 8px = translate-y-2 is correct.

Actually re-checking: the bars have h-0.5 = 2px. gap-1.5 = 6px. So from top of first bar to top of second bar = 2+6 = 8px. From top of second to top of third = 2+6 = 8px. The top bar needs to move down by 8px (translate-y-2 = 8px ✓) and the bottom bar needs to move up by 8px (-translate-y-2 = -8px ✓).

The real issue: the translate happens on the bar itself, but the parent uses flexbox with gap. When the middle bar becomes opacity-0, it still takes up space in the flex layout, so the top and bottom bars dont actually need to account for the gap collapsing.

Fix: Change the button from flex with gap to relative positioning so the bars overlap correctly when transformed:

```tsx
<button
  className="md:hidden relative w-8 h-8"
  onClick={() => setMenuOpen((prev) => !prev)}
  aria-label="Toggle menu"
>
  <span
    className={`absolute left-1.5 top-[10px] block w-5 h-0.5 bg-black transition-all duration-200 ${
      menuOpen ? "rotate-45 translate-y-[6px]" : ""
    }`}
  />
  <span
    className={`absolute left-1.5 top-[16px] block w-5 h-0.5 bg-black transition-all duration-200 ${
      menuOpen ? "opacity-0" : ""
    }`}
  />
  <span
    className={`absolute left-1.5 top-[22px] block w-5 h-0.5 bg-black transition-all duration-200 ${
      menuOpen ? "-rotate-45 -translate-y-[6px]" : ""
    }`}
  />
</button>
```

This positions bars at 10px, 16px, 22px (6px apart). When open, top moves down 6px to 16px center, bottom moves up 6px to 16px center, forming a perfect X.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the mobile dropdown menu
- Do NOT change desktop nav
- Do NOT change nav links data","acceptance_criteria":"Run `npm run build` — succeeds. On mobile viewport: hamburger shows 3 evenly spaced horizontal bars. When clicked, bars animate into a centered X shape with no visible offset or misalignment.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"holke.xyz","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-02-23T19:55:05Z","created_by":"holke.xyz","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:55:05Z","labels":["scope:small"]}

## What to do
Navigation links dont indicate the current page for screen readers. Since this is currently a single-page site (only "/" exists), add aria-current="page" to indicate the active state.

Use the Next.js `usePathname` hook to determine the current path and set aria-current dynamically.

1. Add import: `import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";`
2. Inside the Header component, add: `const pathname = usePathname();`
3. For each Link in both desktop and mobile nav, add the aria-current attribute:

```tsx
<Link
  key={link.label}
  href={link.href}
  className="text-sm text-black hover:opacity-70 transition-opacity"
  aria-current={pathname === link.href ? "page" : undefined}
>
```

External <a> tags dont need this since they navigate away from the site.

Also add aria-current to the logo Link:
```tsx

- components/Guilloche.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace the `buildRadialPaths` function with a new `buildSpirographPaths` function that generates hypotrochoid curves with wavy edges matching the Figma brand guilloché.

### Math: Hypotrochoid with perturbation
The base curve is a hypotrochoid:
```
x(t) = (R - r) * cos(t) + d * cos((R - r) / r * t)
y(t) = (R - r) * sin(t) - d * sin((R - r) / r * t)
```
Where R = outer radius, r = inner radius, d = pen distance.

Add a waviness perturbation to create the undulating edges seen in the Figma:
```
// Perturb the radius with a high-frequency sine
perturbation = waviness * sin(waveFreq * t)
x(t) = ((R - r) + perturbation) * cos(t) + d * cos((R - r) / r * t)
y(t) = ((R - r) + perturbation) * sin(t) - d * sin((R - r) / r * t)
```

### Function signature
```ts
interface SpirographLayer {
  R: number;       // outer radius (fraction of viewBox, 0-1)
  r: number;       // inner radius (fraction of viewBox, 0-1)
  d: number;       // pen distance (fraction of viewBox, 0-1)
  rotation: number; // rotation in degrees
  waviness: number; // amplitude of edge perturbation (0 = smooth, higher = wavier)
  waveFreq: number; // frequency of edge perturbation
}

function buildSpirographPath(
  layer: SpirographLayer,
  width: number,
  height: number,
  steps?: number
): string
```

The function should:
1. Compute the hypotrochoid with perturbation at `steps` points (default 360)
2. Center the curve at (width/2, height/2)
3. Scale to fit within the viewBox using the R parameter
4. Apply the rotation angle
5. Return a single SVG path `d` string using M and L commands (no curves needed at 360 steps)

### Keep the old function
Rename `buildRadialPaths` to `buildLegacyRadialPaths` and keep it. The `variant="radial"` should now call the new spirograph function. Add a new variant `"legacy-radial"` that calls the old function, so nothing breaks if needed.

### Keep buildWavePaths unchanged
The wave variant must remain identical.
